text.title: django-api Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-api as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Services called from Celery tasks or management commands have no request object. Pass user_id or the User model instance instead.

failure-mode.description: N+1 queries in get_queryset without select_related or prefetch_related.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ViewSet.list() iterates the queryset. Without prefetch, each serialized related object issues an additional query. Use django-debug-toolbar to catch this in development.

failure-mode.description: Using force_authenticate(user=user) in all tests without any unauthenticated tests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: force_authenticate bypasses permission classes. Tests stay green even when permissions are misconfigured. Always include at least one test asserting 401 or 403 for each endpoint.

failure-mode.description: Treating PUT as equivalent to PATCH and sending partial updates to PUT endpoints.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ModelViewSet.update defaults to PUT (full replace). Sending partial data results in unset fields being nulled. Use PATCH for partial updates and patch() in tests.

failure-mode.description: Missing tags= in @extend_schema. All endpoints grouped under "default" in Swagger UI.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without tags, Swagger UI lists every endpoint in one undifferentiated group, making the docs unusable for APIs with more than 10 endpoints.
